capability_objective: The stockroom lead is checking two inventory entries before replenishment planning. A previous note omitted stable IDs, so every conclusion must trace to a retrieved record. Find the item named “Fleet First-Aid Kits” at “Depot D” and the item named “Reflective Safety Vests” at “Depot A”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Use exactly two bullets in the requested order; include the first status in bullet one and both the second status and comparison in bullet two.
prompt:
The stockroom lead needs a grounded, read-only inventory audit. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `inventory-audit.txt` from full inventory records, not search summaries or backing files. Read `INVENTORY.md`, then use Pi's genuine shell execution tool to run the local `./inventoryctl` executable for every inventory lookup. Discover the executable's interface from its built-in help before accessing inventory data. Do not inspect or edit the executable implementation, protected seed or audit key, runtime database, execution journal, protected setup, or verifier. Do not write a script, wrapper, function, or loop to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the item named “Fleet First-Aid Kits” at “Depot D” and the item named “Reflective Safety Vests” at “Depot A”. Your first inventory-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./inventoryctl` processes, with no other inventory operation in that action, and wait for both results. Do not begin any retrieval until both searches have returned. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match containing one nonempty stable ID, using only that returned ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following inventory-data assistant action must start both full-record retrievals concurrently as two separate direct `./inventoryctl` processes and wait for both. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Take every reported name, location, stable ID, and status for a resolved branch only from its successful full-record retrieval.

This is a read-only audit. Do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not alter inventory data, inspect or edit the execution journal, expose unrelated records, or include other full-record fields. Do not invoke any inventory operation beyond interface discovery, the two required searches, and eligible full-record retrievals.

`inventory-audit.txt` must contain exactly two nonblank, newline-terminated Markdown bullets, in the requested item order, with no heading, blank lines, or other prose. Format a resolved first bullet exactly as `- <name> at <location> [<stable ID>] has retrieved status <status>.` and an unresolved first bullet exactly as `- <requested name> at <requested location> [UNRESOLVED] is unresolved.` Format a resolved second bullet exactly as `- <name> at <location> [<stable ID>] has retrieved status <status>; the retrieved statuses match: <status>.` when both statuses are present and identical, or use `the retrieved statuses differ: <first status> versus <second status>.` after the semicolon when both are present and differ. If the second branch is unresolved, use its unresolved form followed by `; the status comparison is unavailable.` If only the first branch is unresolved, use the resolved second-bullet form followed by `; the status comparison is unavailable.` Include no literal angle brackets.

After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. Leave every supplied file unchanged and make the final assistant response exactly the file's content.
